claim: Tokens are roughly 3/4 of a word for English text; context window size is the hard limit on how much conversation history and external data an LLM can process at once
actionable: When designing agents, account for token limits in context windows; calculate document sizes needed (500GB = millions of documents, too large for single context window)
source_title: Don't learn AI Agents without Learning these Fundamentals

claim: Different models have drastically different context window sizes: GPT-4.1 and Gemini 2.5 Pro support up to 1M tokens, while nano/mini/flash models cap at 2-4K tokens
actionable: Choose models based on context requirements: large windows for document understanding, small models for low-latency simple queries
source_title: Don't learn AI Agents without Learning these Fundamentals

claim: LLMs can be memory-bound by irrelevant context; including information unrelated to the question (like taste descriptions when counting apples) degrades performance
actionable: Filter and clean retrieved context before passing to LLM; irrelevant information in the context window confuses models and wastes tokens
source_title: Don't learn AI Agents without Learning these Fundamentals

claim: Embeddings transform text into vectors (~1536 dimensions) such that semantically similar phrases end up mathematically close; this enables semantic search
actionable: Use embedding models to convert documents and queries into vectors; then use cosine similarity to find relevant documents rather than keyword matching
source_title: Don't learn AI Agents without Learning these Fundamentals

claim: Semantic similarity via embeddings finds documents by meaning rather than keywords; 'vacation policy' and 'staff time off guidelines' are matched despite different wording
actionable: Build semantic search systems using embeddings instead of keyword search; this handles synonyms and paraphrases automatically
source_title: Don't learn AI Agents without Learning these Fundamentals

claim: LangChain reduces boilerplate by 70%: OpenAI SDK requires 10+ lines to call a model; LangChain does it in 3 lines with provider-agnostic interfaces
actionable: Use LangChain for AI application development to avoid rewriting code when switching between OpenAI, Anthropic, and Google models
source_title: Don't learn AI Agents without Learning these Fundamentals

claim: Switching AI providers with LangChain is trivial: change one line (llm = ChatAnthropic) instead of rewriting authentication and API handling
actionable: Structure AI code using LangChain abstractions to enable provider flexibility; document the key model selection line for easy swaps
source_title: Don't learn AI Agents without Learning these Fundamentals

claim: Prompt templates with placeholders eliminate maintaining hundreds of slightly different prompts; use f-string-like variable substitution for reusable prompts
actionable: Create template-based prompts with placeholders instead of hardcoding; reuse templates across different inputs and contexts
source_title: Don't learn AI Agents without Learning these Fundamentals

claim: Output parsers transform free-text LLM responses into structured objects (JSON, lists); this makes downstream code processing reliable and type-safe
actionable: Chain LLM outputs through parsers to extract structured data; don't process raw text directly
source_title: Don't learn AI Agents without Learning these Fundamentals

claim: LangChain pipes chain multiple components: prompt→model→parser→output in one line using pipe operator, reducing code complexity
actionable: Use LangChain's pipe operator to compose AI pipelines; chain prompts, models, and parsers together for cleaner, more maintainable code
source_title: Don't learn AI Agents without Learning these Fundamentals

claim: Zero-shot prompting asks AI to perform tasks without examples; relies entirely on training knowledge without task-specific guidance
actionable: Use zero-shot prompting for well-established tasks where training data is abundant; provide specific constraints to guide output
source_title: Don't learn AI Agents without Learning these Fundamentals

claim: One-shot and few-shot prompting provide template examples for AI to follow; replicates formatting and style without explicit instructions
actionable: Include 1-3 examples in prompts to establish format and tone; this ensures consistency across outputs better than descriptive instructions alone
source_title: Don't learn AI Agents without Learning these Fundamentals

claim: Chain-of-thought prompting asks AI to show reasoning step-by-step instead of direct answers; improves accuracy on complex reasoning tasks
actionable: For complex tasks, explicitly ask model to show reasoning steps and intermediate conclusions before final answer
source_title: Don't learn AI Agents without Learning these Fundamentals

claim: Vector databases store meaning (embeddings) not keywords; 500GB of documents requires chunking since even 1M token context fits only ~50 business documents
actionable: Implement vector databases with chunked documents for large knowledge bases; don't try to fit entire document sets into context windows
source_title: Don't learn AI Agents without Learning these Fundamentals

claim: RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) retrieves relevant documents, augments prompt with that context, then generates answer; this avoids hallucination on private data
actionable: Build RAG pipelines for domain-specific knowledge: embed documents, search by semantic similarity, inject context into prompt, generate grounded answers
source_title: Don't learn AI Agents without Learning these Fundamentals

claim: Chunking strategies differ by document type: legal docs need paragraph-level chunks to preserve structure; conversation transcripts can use sentence-level chunks with high overlap
actionable: Tailor chunk size and overlap to document type; preserve semantic units (legal paragraphs vs conversational sentences)
source_title: Don't learn AI Agents without Learning these Fundamentals

claim: Semantic search (via embeddings) solves vocabulary mismatch: 'forgot my password' matches 'account recovery process' through meaning, not keywords
actionable: Use embedding-based semantic search instead of regex/SQL LIKE queries for document retrieval
source_title: Don't learn AI Agents without Learning these Fundamentals

claim: LangGraph extends LangChain with stateful multi-step workflows, conditional branching, and loops; essential for complex reasoning tasks
actionable: Use LangGraph for multi-step AI workflows requiring intermediate decisions, loops, or conditional routing
source_title: Don't learn AI Agents without Learning these Fundamentals

claim: State graphs maintain shared state across all nodes in a workflow; nodes read/write state, enabling context to persist through multi-step reasoning
actionable: Design workflows using state graphs to track intermediate results; state persists across conditionals and loops
source_title: Don't learn AI Agents without Learning these Fundamentals

claim: Conditional routing in graphs enables adaptive workflows: inspect state and route to different nodes based on intermediate results
actionable: Implement conditional edges in workflow graphs to enable adaptive branching; use intermediate results to decide next steps
source_title: Don't learn AI Agents without Learning these Fundamentals

claim: MCP (Model Context Protocol) acts as a universal interface for AI agents to access external tools, databases, and APIs without custom integrations
actionable: Use MCP servers to expose tools and databases to AI agents; this enables plug-and-play integrations without rewriting agent code
source_title: Don't learn AI Agents without Learning these Fundamentals

claim: MCP servers expose self-describing tool interfaces; agents understand input/output types autonomously without manual integration code
actionable: Design MCP servers with clear schema definitions; agents can discover and use tools without hardcoded integration details
source_title: Don't learn AI Agents without Learning these Fundamentals

claim: Agents differ from static LLMs by having autonomy, memory, and tools; agents self-determine how to solve problems vs. answering static questions
actionable: Design systems as agents rather than simple query-response LLMs; equip agents with tools, memory, and decision-making autonomy
source_title: Don't learn AI Agents without Learning these Fundamentals

claim: Document chunking with overlap preserves context at chunk boundaries; 100-char overlap on 500-char chunks improves retrieval accuracy by ~40%
actionable: Implement overlapping chunks when splitting documents; empirically tune overlap amount based on average semantic distance across boundaries
source_title: Don't learn AI Agents without Learning these Fundamentals

claim: Scoring threshold for vector similarity prevents false matches; Florida might have surface similarity to vacation but should not match IT policy queries
actionable: Set similarity score thresholds to prevent false matches; test on domain-specific queries to find optimal threshold
source_title: Don't learn AI Agents without Learning these Fundamentals
